---
title: "FInal Project Sepsis survival rates"
author: "JN"
date: "2026-02-19"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:
```{r}
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "rio", "car", "pwr", "MOTE", "Hmisc", "corrplot", "psych", "ppcor", "gh", "devtools", "remotes", "ggplot2"))    
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, rio, ez, MOTE, ggplot2, gridExtra)

```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)      # ggplot2, dplyr
library(rio)            # import() function
library(car)            # vif(), influencePlot()
library(corrplot)       # Visual correlation matrices
library(psych)          # Descriptive statistics
library(ppcor)          # spcor(), ppcor() for partial correlations
library(knitr)          # Markdown tables
library(kableExtra) 
library(pwr)       # For power analysis
library(MOTE)      # For effect size calculations
library(Hmisc)     # For plotting error bars
library(gridExtra)
```

```{r}
data <- read.csv("sepsis-primary-norway.csv")
str(data)
summary(data)
psych::describe(data)
dim(data)
korean <- read.csv("data/Sepsis-validation-Korea.csv")
str(korean)
summary(korean)
psych::describe(korean)
dim(korean)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
p1 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = age_years)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "orange", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of age Norway",
       subtitle = "All ages are within the range 0-100 ",
       x = "age", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

# Boxplot to see any outliers clearly
p2 <- ggplot(korean, aes(x = age_years)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "orange", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of age Korean",
       subtitle = "All ages are within the range 20-89 ",
       x = "age", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
```



## Accuracy


```{r }
noout <- data # Create a copy to update

p3 <- ggplot(noout, aes(y = age_years)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of age",
       subtitle = "The data at bottom appears to be extreme",
       y = "age") +
  theme_minimal()

# Boxplot to see any outliers clearly
p4 <- ggplot(korean, aes(y = age_years)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "orange", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Boxplot of age",
       subtitle = "The data appears to have a good spread",
       y = "age") +
  theme_minimal()

grid.arrange(p3, p4, ncol = 2)
```


\



#Renamed column names

```{r}
colnames(noout) <- c("age", "gender", "episodes", "survival")
colnames(korean) <- c("age", "gender", "episodes", "observed_survival")

```


#Missing values check
```{r}
apply(noout, 2, function(x) {sum(is.na(x)) })
```

##Outliers and duplicate values check

```{r}

sum(duplicated(data))
nrow(unique(noout))

```

# Descriptive statistics

```{r}
norway_summary <- noout %>%
  group_by(gender) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_survival = mean(survival, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

norway_summary
ggplot(norway_summary,
       aes(x = gender, y = mean_survival)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_survival, 2)),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(title = "Average Survival Rate by Sex – Norway Cohort",
       x = "Sex",
       y = "Survival Rate") +
  theme_clean
```

```{r}
korean_summary <- korean %>%
  group_by(gender) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_survival = mean(observed_survival, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

korean_summary
ggplot(korean_summary,
       aes(x = gender, y = mean_survival)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue", width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_survival, 2)),
            vjust = -0.5,
            size = 5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(title = "Average Survival Rate by Sex – Norway Cohort",
       x = "Sex",
       y = "Survival Rate") +
  theme_clean
```

#Corelation matrix 

```{r}
corr_matrix <- cor(noout[, c("survival", "age", "gender", "episodes")])
round(corr_matrix, 2)
corrplot(corr_matrix,
         method = "color",
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.srt = 45)

```
#Logrithimic regression model fitting
```{r}
model_logit<- glm(survival ~ age + gender + episodes,
                  family=binomial, data = noout)
summary(model_logit)
```
#Odd's ratio and confidence interval summary
```{r}
# Coefficients
model_summary <- summary(model_logit)
coef_table <- model_summary$coefficients

# Odds ratios
OR <- exp(coef(model_logit))

# 95% CI
CI <- exp(confint(model_logit))  # profile likelihood CI

# Combine into dataframe
results_table <- data.frame(
  Predictor = rownames(coef_table),
  Estimate  = coef_table[,1],
  Std_Error = coef_table[,2],
  OR        = OR,
  CI_Lower  = CI[,1],
  CI_Upper  = CI[,2],
  p_value   = coef_table[,4]
)
results_table <- results_table[-1, ]

library(kableExtra)

kable(results_table,row.names = FALSE,
      digits = 3,
      caption = "Logistic Regression Results: Odds Ratios and 95% CI") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```



```{r}
library(car)
vif(model_logit)

```

```{r}
summary(hatvalues(model_logit))
summary(cooks.distance(model_logit))

```
```{r}
# Get predicted probabilities
noout$predicted_prob <- predict(model_logit, type = "response")
head(noout)
```

```{r}
noout$risk_group <- cut(
  noout$predicted_prob,
  breaks = quantile(noout$predicted_prob,
                    probs = c(0, .33, .66, 1)),
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c("High Risk", "Medium Risk", "Low Risk")
)

```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)

library(dplyr)

noout %>%
  group_by(risk_group) %>%
  summarise(mean_survival = mean(survival))

 
theme_clean <- theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )


ggplot(risk_summary,
       aes(x = risk_group,
           y = survival,
           fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Observed Survival Rate by Risk groups",
       x = "Risk Group",
       y = "Observed Survival Probability") +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")+
  theme_clean



```






```{r}
# refit model using model_logit and estimate the predicted probability in the Korean dataset

korean$predicted_prob_survival <- predict(model_logit,
                                 newdata = korean,
                                 type = "response")
head(korean)

```

#Assign Korean patients into risk groups using the training cutoff

```{r}
train_probs <- predict(model_logit,
                       type = "response")

train_cutoffs <- quantile(train_probs,
                          probs = c(0, .33, .66, 1))

korean$risk_group <- cut(korean$predicted_prob_survival,
                         breaks = train_cutoffs, 
                         labels =  c("Low","Medium","High"),
                         include.lowest = TRUE)

head(korean)

```

```{r}
mean(korean$predicted_prob_survival)
mean(korean$observed_survival)

```





```{r}
korean_risk_summary_predicted <- aggregate(predicted_prob_survival ~ risk_group,
          data = korean,
          mean)
head(korean_risk_summary_predicted)

korean_risk_summary_observed <- aggregate(observed_survival ~ risk_group,
          data = korean,
          mean)
head(korean_risk_summary_observed)
```




```{r}
library (ggplot2)
p1 <- ggplot(korean_risk_summary_predicted ,
       aes(x = risk_group,
           y = predicted_prob_survival,
             fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Predicted Survival for Validation data",
       x = "Risk Group",
       y = "Predicted Probability of survival") +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")+
  theme_clean

p2 <- ggplot(korean_risk_summary_observed ,
       aes(x = risk_group,
           y = observed_survival,
             fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(title = "Observed Survival for Validation data",
       x = "Risk Group",
       y = "Observed survival") +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  guides(fill = "none")+
  theme_clean

library(gridExtra)
grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)

```
